## SECTION 19 — This Day in History (Global)
*   1521: Ferdinand Magellan was killed in the Battle of Mactan in the Philippines.
*   1865: The steamboat SS Sultana exploded on the Mississippi River, killing an estimated 1,800 people in one of the worst maritime disasters in US history.
*   1960: Togo gained independence from French administration.
*   1981: Xerox PARC introduced the computer mouse.
*   1994: South Africa held its first multiracial general election, marking the end of apartheid. Nelson Mandela was elected president.
*   2005: The Airbus A380, the world's largest passenger airliner, made its first flight from Toulouse, France.
*   2011: The largest outbreak of tornadoes in US history occurred across the Southern, Midwestern, and Eastern United States.
*   2014: Pope John XXIII and Pope John Paul II were canonized by Pope Francis in a rare dual canonization ceremony.

---

## SECTION 20 — This Day in History (India)
*   1748: The Treaty of Aix-la-Chapelle was signed, ending the War of the Austrian Succession and restoring Madras to the British East India Company.
*   1948: The Indian Parliament passed the Minimum Wages Act.
*   1950: The Planning Commission of India was established.
*   1960: The states of Maharashtra and Gujarat were formed.
*   1982: India launched its first indigenous communication satellite, INSAT-1A, though it failed to achieve orbit.
*   1989: The Supreme Court of India delivered a landmark judgment recognizing the right to live with dignity as part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution.
